title: Temperature controlled transport puck
abstract: One embodiment provides a method of controlling a payload temperature in in vitro diagnostics including: moving, using a track system, a plurality of carriers, along one or more paths between a plurality of testing stations, wherein the carrier is configured to hold one or more payloads, and limiting, using a temperature controlling device, temperature change of the one or more payloads, wherein each carrier is configured to hold one or more payloads and move the one or more payloads to one of the plurality of testing stations. Other aspects are described and claimed herein.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  We claim: 1. A temperature control system for use in in vitro diagnostics comprising: a plurality of carriers that utilize a track system configured to provide one or more paths between a plurality of testing stations; and a passive temperature controlling device connected to each carrier, wherein each carrier is configured to hold one or more payloads and move the one or more payloads to one of the plurality of testing stations, and wherein the passive temperature controlling device limits temperature change of the one or more payload. 2. The system of claim 1, wherein the passive temperature control device comprises at least one of: silica aerogel, polyurethane, and polystyrene. 3. The system of claim 1, wherein each carrier comprises one or more magnetic objects, and wherein the track system comprises a coil system, the coil system imparting motion on each carrier using the one or more magnetic objects. 4. The system of claim 3, wherein each carrier is configured to detect at least one of radio-frequency identification tags and barcodes associated with the track, and wherein the imparted motion is determined based on one or more of the at least one of radio-frequency identification tags and barcodes. 5. A temperature control system for use in in vitro diagnostics comprising: a plurality of carriers that utilize a track system configured to provide one or more paths between a plurality of testing stations; and an active temperature controlling device connected to each carrier, wherein each carrier is configured to hold one or more payloads and move the one or more payloads to one of the plurality of testing stations, and wherein the active temperature controlling device limits temperature change of the one or more payload. 6. The system of claim 5, wherein the active temperature control device comprises at least one of: a thermoelectric cooling device and an electrocaloric cooling device. 7. The system of claim 6, further comprising a temperature sensor to monitor the temperature of the one or more payloads, wherein the active temperature controlling device is enabled responsive to the temperature sensor detecting a payload temperature below a predetermined threshold. 8. The system of claim 7, wherein each carrier is configured to detect at least one of radio-frequency identification tags and barcodes to determine a current track location, and wherein the active temperature controlling device is enabled based on the current location. 9. The system of claim 6, wherein each carrier comprises one or more magnetic objects, and wherein the track system comprises a coil system, 

 the coil system imparting motion on each carrier using the one or more magnetic objects. 10. The system of claim 5, wherein each carrier is configured to detect at least one of radio-frequency identification tags and barcodes associated with the track, and wherein the imparted motion is determined based on one or more of the at least one of radio-frequency identification tags and barcodes. 11. The system of claim 5, further comprising a remote device, wherein the active temperature controlling device is enabled and disabled from the remote device. 12. A method of controlling a payload temperature in in vitro diagnostics comprising: moving, using a track system, a plurality of carriers, along one or more paths between a plurality of testing stations, wherein the carrier is configured to hold one or more payloads, and limiting, using a temperature controlling device, temperature change of the one or more payloads, wherein each carrier is configured to hold one or more payloads and move the one or more payloads to one of the plurality of testing stations. 13. The method of claim 12, wherein the temperature controlling device comprises a passive temperature control device. 14. The method of claim 13, wherein the passive temperature control device comprises at least one of: silica aerogel, polyurethane, and polystyrene. 15. The method of claim 12, wherein the temperature controlling device comprises an active temperature control device. 16. The method of claim 15, wherein the active temperature control device comprises at least one of: a thermoelectric cooling device and an electrocaloric cooling device. 17. The method of claim 15, further comprising monitoring, using a temperature sensor, the temperature of the one or more payloads, wherein the active temperature controlling device is enabled responsive to the temperature sensor detecting a payload temperature below a predetermined threshold. 18. The method of claim 15 further comprising activating, using a remote device, the active temperature controlling device. 19. The method of claim 12, wherein each carrier comprises one or more magnetic objects, and wherein the track system comprises a coil system, the coil system imparting motion on each carrier using the one or more magnetic objects. 20. The method of claim 19, wherein each carrier is configured to detect at least one of radio-frequency identification tags and barcodes associated with the track, and wherein the imparted motion is determined based on one or more of the at least one of radio-frequency identification tags and barcodes.
